output:
CRITICAL - Code Discovery Protocol:
1. ALWAYS use codebase-memory-mcp tools FIRST for ANY code exploration:
   - search_graph(name_pattern/label/qn_pattern) to find functions/classes/routes
   - trace_path(function_name, mode=calls|data_flow|cross_service) for call chains
   - get_code_snippet(qualified_name) to read source (NOT Read/cat)
   - query_graph(query) for complex Cypher patterns
   - get_architecture(aspects) for project structure
   - search_code(pattern) for text search (graph-augmented grep)
2. Fall back to Grep/Glob/Read ONLY for text content, config values, non-code files.
3. If a project is not indexed yet, run index_repository FIRST.
stdout:
CRITICAL - Code Discovery Protocol:
1. ALWAYS use codebase-memory-mcp tools FIRST for ANY code exploration:
   - search_graph(name_pattern/label/qn_pattern) to find functions/classes/routes
   - trace_path(function_name, mode=calls|data_flow|cross_service) for call chains
   - get_code_snippet(qualified_name) to read source (NOT Read/cat)
   - query_graph(query) for complex Cypher patterns
   - get_architecture(aspects) for project structure
   - search_code(pattern) for text search (graph-augmented grep)
2. Fall back to Grep/Glob/Read ONLY for text content, config values, non-code files.
3. If a project is not indexed yet, run index_repository FIRST.

content.content:
89	type Node struct {
90		// NodeHash is the content-addressed identity: sha256(repoURL || packagePath || symbolName || symbolKind).
91		// Note: contentHash was removed from the computation; node identity
92		// depends only on (repo, package, name, kind).
93		NodeHash      Hash
94		FileHash      Hash   // reference to the containing File record
95		QualifiedName string // fully qualified name: "{repoURL}://{pkgPath}.{TypeName}.{SymbolName}"
96		Kind          string // one of: function, type, method, interface, const, var
97		Line          int    // 1-indexed source line number of the declaration
98		Signature     string // human-readable type signature for display (e.g., "func (SQLiteStore) PutNode()")
99		Doc           string // doc comment preceding the declaration (first 200 chars, language-agnostic)
100		LastAuthor    string  // git blame: author of the last commit that touched this symbol's line
101		LastCommitAt  int64   // git blame: unix timestamp of the last commit that touched this symbol's line
102		CoveragePct   float64 // test coverage percentage for this symbol's lines (0.0-100.0, -1 = not measured)
103	}
104	
105	// Edge represents a directed relationship between two nodes in the knowledge
106	// graph. Edge types include "calls", "imports", "implements", and "references".
107	// Each edge carries a confidence score and provenance tag indicating how it
108	// was derived (ast_resolved, ast_inferred, lsp_resolved, etc.).
109	type Edge struct {
110		EdgeHash   Hash    // content-addressed identity: sha256(sourceHash || targetHash || edgeType || provenance)
111		SourceHash Hash    // hash of the source node (caller, importer, implementor)
112		TargetHash Hash    // hash of the target node (callee, imported package, interface)
113		EdgeType   string  // relationship kind: "calls", "imports", "implements", "references"
114		Confidence float64 // quality score from 0.0 to 1.0; ast_inferred=0.7, lsp_resolved=0.9, ast_resolved=1.0
115		Provenance string  // how the edge was derived: "ast_resolved", "ast_inferred", "lsp_resolved", etc.
116		// CallSite fields store the source location of the call expression (not the
117		// target declaration). These positions are used by LSP enrichment: the enricher
118		// sends GetDefinition at (CallSiteFile, CallSiteLine, CallSiteCol) to confirm
119		// or correct the target. Zero values mean no call-site info is available.
120		CallSiteLine int    // 1-indexed line of the call expression in the source file
121		CallSiteCol  int    // 0-indexed column of the call expression
122		CallSiteFile string // relative file path (within the repo) containing the call expression
123		// Runtime observation fields. Zero values for static edges.
124		ObservationCount int   // total observations in current window (0 for static edges)
125		LastObserved     int64 // unix timestamp of last observation (0 for static edges)
126	}
127	
128	// File represents a tracked source file within a repository. The FileHash

content.text:
That's comprehensive. Here's the answer:

## Impact of changing `types.Node`

The `Node` struct (at `internal/types/types.go:89`) is the **central data type** in the codebase. Changing it touches **~100 files** across virtually every package.

### Fields

```go
NodeHash, FileHash Hash
QualifiedName, Kind, Signature, Doc, LastAuthor string
Line int
LastCommitAt int64
CoveragePct float64
```

### Packages that directly depend on `types.Node`

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| `internal/store` | SQLite persistence — reads/writes all Node fields |
| `internal/indexer/*` | 17+ extractors — every extractor constructs `types.Node` literals |
| `internal/context` | Context engine — ranks, formats, tokens, explains nodes |
| `internal/mcp` | MCP handlers — serializes nodes into tool responses |
| `internal/embedding` | Vector searcher — indexes nodes |
| `internal/enrichment` | LSP enricher — updates node fields after indexing |
| `internal/diff` | Semantic diff — compares node sets across snapshots |
| `internal/resolver` | Symbol resolution — looks up nodes by hash |
| `internal/snapshot` | Snapshot manager — verifies node integrity |
| `internal/trace` | Dataflow tracing — traverses node/edge graph |
| `cmd/knowing` | CLI commands (`testscope`, `context`, `main`) |
| `bench/*` | All benchmarks |
| `e2e_test.go` | End-to-end tests |

### What specifically breaks by change type

- **Add a field**: `internal/store/sqlite.go` (schema + scan queries), `internal/testutil/mockstore.go`, any code that constructs `Node{}` literals (all 17+ extractors, `scipingest`, `treesitter`)
- **Remove a field**: Same as above, plus `internal/context/ranking.go` (uses `CoveragePct`), `internal/context/explain.go` (uses `Doc`, `Signature`), `internal/mcp/server.go` (serializes all fields)
- **Rename a field**: Compiler catches all sites — every extractor, store, context engine, MCP handler, benchmarks
- **Change `NodeHash` type**: Cascades into `Edge.SourceHash`/`TargetHash`, every store query, every hash computation in extractors

**Bottom line:** `types.Node` is a load-bearing struct. Any field change requires coordinated updates across `store`, all extractors, `context`, `mcp`, and `embedding` packages.

That's comprehensive. Here's the answer:

## Impact of changing `types.Node`

The `Node` struct (at `internal/types/types.go:89`) is the **central data type** in the codebase. Changing it touches **~100 files** across virtually every package.

### Fields

```go
NodeHash, FileHash Hash
QualifiedName, Kind, Signature, Doc, LastAuthor string
Line int
LastCommitAt int64
CoveragePct float64
```

### Packages that directly depend on `types.Node`

| Package | Role |
|---|---|
| `internal/store` | SQLite persistence — reads/writes all Node fields |
| `internal/indexer/*` | 17+ extractors — every extractor constructs `types.Node` literals |
| `internal/context` | Context engine — ranks, formats, tokens, explains nodes |
| `internal/mcp` | MCP handlers — serializes nodes into tool responses |
| `internal/embedding` | Vector searcher — indexes nodes |
| `internal/enrichment` | LSP enricher — updates node fields after indexing |
| `internal/diff` | Semantic diff — compares node sets across snapshots |
| `internal/resolver` | Symbol resolution — looks up nodes by hash |
| `internal/snapshot` | Snapshot manager — verifies node integrity |
| `internal/trace` | Dataflow tracing — traverses node/edge graph |
| `cmd/knowing` | CLI commands (`testscope`, `context`, `main`) |
| `bench/*` | All benchmarks |
| `e2e_test.go` | End-to-end tests |

### What specifically breaks by change type

- **Add a field**: `internal/store/sqlite.go` (schema + scan queries), `internal/testutil/mockstore.go`, any code that constructs `Node{}` literals (all 17+ extractors, `scipingest`, `treesitter`)
- **Remove a field**: Same as above, plus `internal/context/ranking.go` (uses `CoveragePct`), `internal/context/explain.go` (uses `Doc`, `Signature`), `internal/mcp/server.go` (serializes all fields)
- **Rename a field**: Compiler catches all sites — every extractor, store, context engine, MCP handler, benchmarks
- **Change `NodeHash` type**: Cascades into `Edge.SourceHash`/`TargetHash`, every store query, every hash computation in extractors

**Bottom line:** `types.Node` is a load-bearing struct. Any field change requires coordinated updates across `store`, all extractors, `context`, `mcp`, and `embedding` packages.
